title: lepidaploa acilepis
intro: Lepidaploa acilepis, also known as the 'Snake Tongue', is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a shrub that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Lepidaploa acilepis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for the treatment of various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties.
